title: Carrot cake
subtitle: Spiced oil cake with grated carrot and cream cheese frosting
excerpt: A deeply spiced, oil-based cake using grated carrot for moisture and natural sweetness. Two layers, filled and topped with a tangy cream cheese frosting. Oil rather than butter keeps the crumb moist for days. Walnuts can be stirred in or scattered on top.
sourceNotes: Carrot cake as a popular British baking item developed in the mid-20th century, partly influenced by wartime recipes that used vegetables as a sugar substitute. The oil-based, spiced formula with cream cheese frosting became widely established in British home baking by the 1980s. The recipe here follows the contemporary standard: plain flour, oil, brown sugar, eggs, two teaspoons of cinnamon, and cream cheese frosting.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unfrosted cake layers individually for up to 2 months. Frost on the day of serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The frosted cake keeps refrigerated for 3 days. Remove from the fridge 30 minutes before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven. Fan at 160°C. The oil-based batter stays moist; check at 30 minutes with a skewer.

recipeTools.notes: two 20 cm round tins, greased and base-lined

glossaryTerms.definition: A cake in which vegetable or sunflower oil replaces butter as the fat. Oil distributes more evenly through the batter than creamed butter and keeps the crumb moist at refrigerator temperatures. Oil-based cakes do not have the creaming step and are therefore simpler to mix. The trade-off is a less pronounced butter flavour, which is compensated by spice or citrus.

content.text: . There is no creaming step and no softened butter; just oil, eggs, and sugar whisked together, then the flour and spices folded in. Grate the carrot on the coarse side of the box grater, not the fine. The moisture from the carrot is part of the recipe's hydration. The cream cheese frosting needs to be cold when it goes on: warm frosting runs.

Heat the oven to 180°C conventional (160°C fan). Grease and base-line two 20 cm round tins. Sift the flour, bicarbonate, baking powder, spices, and salt into a large bowl. In a separate bowl, whisk the caster sugar, light brown sugar, and oil together until combined. Add the eggs one at a time, whisking after each. Pour the wet mixture into the dry and fold together until just combined. Fold in the grated carrot and walnuts if using.

Divide between the two prepared tins and bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the surface springs back to a gentle press and a skewer comes out clean. Leave in the tins for 10 minutes, then cool completely on a rack.

For the frosting, beat the softened butter until pale. Add the cold cream cheese and beat briefly until smooth; do not overbeat, which causes the frosting to slacken. Add the icing sugar in two additions, beating on low, then the vanilla. Refrigerate for 30 minutes until spreadable. Sandwich the cooled cake layers with half the frosting; spread the remainder over the top.
